mnemonics.title: Register vs Memory
mnemonics.content: Register: Small, High-Speed Memory Location within CPU; Memory: Larger, Slower Memory Location outside CPU

mnemonics.title: Stack Pointer
mnemonics.content: Points to Top Element of Stack

mnemonics.title: Push vs Pop
mnemonics.content: Push: Add Element to Top of Stack; Pop: Remove Element from Top of Stack

mnemonics.title: Stack Frame
mnemonics.content: Stores Local Variables and Parameters of Function or Procedure

acronyms.usage: Describes the Order in which Elements are Added and Removed from a Stack
Temporary Storage for Data and Programs

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a Stack of Plates, where Each Plate Represents a Data Element
Think of Registers as Small, High-Speed Pockets within the CPU, and Memory as a Larger, Slower Storage Area outside the CPU
Picture a Pointer that Moves Up and Down the Stack, Pointing to the Top Element

analogies.analogy: A Stack of Plates, where Each Plate is Added and Removed from the Top
A Desk Drawer (Register) vs a File Cabinet (Memory)
A Bookmark that Keeps Track of the Current Page in a Book
